plain_definition: Two related types of detailed EU rulebook instrument used throughout MiCA and other EU financial-services law. A Regulatory Technical Standard (RTS) or Implementing Technical Standard (ITS) is drafted by ESMA or EBA under a specific mandate in a Regulation such as MiCA, then submitted to the European Commission, which can endorse it (with limited scope to amend) as a binding EU legal act -- a Commission Delegated Regulation (for an RTS) or a Commission Implementing Regulation (for an ITS). Until the Commission has adopted, and the Official Journal has published, the resulting Regulation, an RTS or ITS is only a finalised draft and is not yet binding law -- a distinction that matters for several MiCA-related technical standards still awaiting Commission endorsement. This instrument type is specific to the EU's own multi-level rulemaking architecture and does not have a direct Hong Kong or UK equivalent.
formal_definition: The RTS/ITS procedure is set out in the Regulations establishing ESMA and EBA (Regulation (EU) No 1095/2010, Articles 10-15; Regulation (EU) No 1093/2010, Articles 10-15): the relevant European Supervisory Authority submits a draft RTS or ITS to the European Commission, which may endorse it, in whole, in part, or with amendments, as a delegated or implementing Regulation.
